tasks.title: Build quest completability validator that checks against actual world data
tasks.description: shared/quest-completability-validator.ts exists and verifies that objective types have event mappings and completion handlers, but it does NOT check whether the actual world data supports completing each quest. Build a world-aware validator. (1) Create shared/quest-feasibility-validator.ts (or extend existing) that takes a full WorldIR and validates every quest against it. (2) For each quest, check every objective: if objective type is 'talk_to_npc' with npcId='X', verify that character X exists in WorldIR.entities.characters. If objective type is 'visit_location' with locationName='Y', verify a building or location named Y exists. If objective type is 'collect_item' with itemName='Z', verify that item Z exists in WorldIR.entities or loot tables. If objective type is 'collect_text' with textId='T', verify document T exists. (3) For conversation-based objectives, verify the target NPC has a dialogue context entry. (4) For crafting objectives, verify the recipe exists and ingredients are obtainable. (5) For location-based objectives, verify the location has a position in the world. (6) Return a report: { feasible: Quest[], infeasible: Quest[], warnings: string[] } with specific reasons for each infeasible quest (e.g., 'Quest X references NPC Y who does not exist'). (7) Add unit tests with mock WorldIR data.

tasks.title: Create quest dependency graph validator
tasks.description: Quests can have prerequisites (questChainOrder, dependsOn), CEFR gates, and chapter requirements. Validate the dependency graph is solvable. (1) Build a dependency graph from all quests: nodes = quests, edges = prerequisites/chain order. (2) Check for cycles (quest A requires B, B requires A). (3) Check for unreachable quests (quest requires a prerequisite that itself is infeasible). (4) Verify that the main quest chain is fully connected from start to finish with no gaps. (5) Verify that CEFR-gated quests are reachable: there must be enough quests at each CEFR level to allow the player to progress to the next level before encountering the gate. (6) Check that chapter progression is possible: chapter N's objectives must be completable using content available at chapter N's CEFR level. (7) Verify that the quest chain has a terminal quest that triggers game completion. (8) Output a visual dependency graph (text-based or mermaid format) for debugging.

tasks.title: Add world data completeness checks to the export pipeline
tasks.description: Before a game is exported, validate that all required data is present and consistent. (1) In the export pipeline (server/services/game-export/), add a pre-export validation step that runs the quest feasibility validator and dependency graph validator. (2) Also validate: every NPC referenced by a quest has a dialogue context entry, every building referenced has a position and geometry spec, every item referenced exists in the items list or loot tables, the main quest chain is complete (no dangling references), the world has at least one settlement with buildings/NPCs, text documents (if any) have valid content. (3) If validation fails, show warnings in the export dialog: 'Warning: 3 quests reference NPCs that don't exist. These quests will be uncompletable.' (4) Allow the user to proceed with warnings or fix issues first. (5) For critical failures (no main quest, no NPCs), block the export with an error. (6) Log the validation report in the exported game's data/ directory as validation-report.json for debugging.

tasks.title: Build automated playability test suite
tasks.description: Create automated tests that simulate gameplay to verify quest completability. (1) Create tests/game-playability.test.ts that loads a generated WorldIR and simulates a player completing quests. (2) For each quest, the test should: check prerequisites are met (or simulate completing them), check that all objectives have reachable completion conditions, simulate the completion events (emit the required GameEventBus events), verify that QuestCompletionEngine marks the quest as complete. (3) Test the full main quest chain: simulate a player going through all 6 chapters, completing each objective type. (4) Test edge cases: what happens if an NPC dies mid-quest? What if a required item is sold? What if the player's CEFR level doesn't progress? (5) Test that side quests don't block main quest progression. (6) Generate a playability report: 'X of Y quests are completable, Z quests have issues: [list]'. (7) Run this test suite as part of the CI/CD pipeline on world generation changes.

tasks.title: Add in-game quest debugging tools
tasks.description: For development and testing, add tools that help verify quest state during gameplay. (1) Add a debug overlay (toggleable with a key combo like Ctrl+Shift+D) that shows: current quest objectives with their completion state, recent GameEventBus events (last 20), active quest chains and progress, CEFR level and proficiency metrics. (2) Add a 'Complete Objective' debug button next to each objective in the debug overlay that simulates the completion event. (3) Add a 'Warp to Quest Location' button that teleports the player to the location referenced by a quest objective. (4) Add quest state logging: when a quest event is emitted, log it to the console with the full event data and which objectives it matched. (5) Add a validation panel that runs the quest feasibility validator against the current game state and highlights issues. (6) All debug tools should be disabled in production builds (check for a debug flag in the game config or environment variable). (7) These tools should also work in exported games when launched with a --debug flag.

tasks.title: Create game world health dashboard in the Insimul editor
tasks.description: World creators need to see at a glance whether their world is ready for export. (1) Add a 'World Health' card to the world editor dashboard that shows: total quests (completable/total), main quest chain status (complete chain or broken), NPC coverage (NPCs with vs without dialogue contexts), building coverage (buildings with vs without interiors), document coverage (documents placed vs unplaced), CEFR distribution (quests per level). (2) Each metric should be green (good), yellow (warning), or red (critical). (3) Clicking a metric drills down to the specific issues: clicking 'Quests: 140/145 completable' shows the 5 infeasible quests with reasons. (4) Add a 'Run Full Validation' button that runs all validators and shows a comprehensive report. (5) Add a 'Fix Issues' button for auto-fixable problems (e.g., generate missing dialogue contexts, reassign quests with missing NPCs to existing NPCs). (6) Show the health dashboard prominently before export — block export if any red metrics exist.
